subject.description: Black male, early 30s, natural hair, slight stubble, wearing a plain grey zip-up hoodie (slightly unzipped). Real skin texture — visible pores, slight shine on forehead from room temperature, no makeup, no grooming. Expression: looking directly into the phone camera, one eyebrow slightly raised, the corners of the mouth pulled very slightly down — the 'I have to tell you what just happened' face. Not performing — this is genuinely what this face looks like when you're about to explain something absurd to someone.
subject.action: Holding the phone himself, recording a voice note or short video. Slight movement in the frame — the natural sway of someone holding their own phone and talking.

setting.location: Standing in a small home office or spare bedroom converted to workspace — a door frame visible behind him, a shelf with random stuff on it (books, a cable, nothing curated). Overhead light. Completely ordinary.
setting.time_of_day: Midday — flat overhead room lighting, no natural light source doing anything interesting.

composition.camera_angle: Slightly low — phone held at chest height, angled up at his face. Natural selfie-recording angle.
composition.framing: Medium close-up — head and upper chest. Top of head fully in frame with minimal headspace. Chin visible with breathing room.

camera_settings.depth_of_field: wide — room behind him mostly in focus

lighting.type: Flat overhead room light — overhead LED or fluorescent. Slightly harsh on top of his head, slight shadow under his chin. Completely uncontrolled.
lighting.color_grading: No color grading. Cool-neutral white balance. Looks exactly like an unedited iPhone video frame.

mood: That very specific pre-rant energy — he has something to say, it's been sitting in him, he picked up his phone to say it. Relatable disbelief, not theatrical anger.
aesthetic_style: Shot on iPhone front-facing camera held at chest level. Grainy sensor noise in the shadows. Slight compression artifacts. Natural phone white balance. Looks like a frame grabbed from a Loom recording or a voice memo video. No post-processing, no filters, no editing app.
